sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Quest Diagnostics (NYSE:DGX) SVP Sells 2,628 Shares
feed.summary: Karthik Kuppusamy, SVP at Quest Diagnostics (NYSE:DGX), sold 2,628 shares of the company's stock on March 4th for over $538,000, reducing his stake by 16.35%. This transaction comes as Quest Diagnostics recently reported strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst estimates, and announced a $1.0 billion share buyback program and an increased quarterly dividend. Analysts currently rate the stock as a "Moderate Buy" with a consensus price target of $210.29.

feed.title: Ex-KEPCO Worker Steals 12km Cables Amid Copper Surge
feed.summary: A former KEPCO employee has been accused of stealing 12 kilometers of copper cables. This theft occurred amid a surge in copper prices, indicating a potential motive for the crime.

feed.title: Incyte Corporation (INCY) Receives FDA Complete Response Letter for Zynyz® sBLA
feed.summary: Incyte Corporation (INCY) received a Complete Response Letter (CRL) from the FDA for its Zynyz® sBLA for metastatic non-small cell lung cancer, citing regulatory compliance issues at a third-party manufacturing facility. The FDA's decision was not based on concerns about the drug's efficacy or safety. Incyte is working with the FDA and the facility to address the issues for a potential resubmission.

feed.title: The Woman Behind a Trouble Call
feed.summary: Alissa Osburn, a troubleman field supervisor at Southern California Edison, shares her 17-year journey in the electric industry, moving from meter reader to substation operator and system operator before her current field role. Despite the male-dominated environment, Osburn advocates for curiosity and determination, encouraging women to explore challenging careers. Her experiences reinforce the value of continuous learning and building trust in specialized fields.

feed.title: Insider Sell: Ralph Larossa Sells Shares of Public Service Enter
feed.summary: Ralph Larossa, Chair, President, and CEO of Public Service Electric and Gas Company, a subsidiary of Public Service Enterprise Group Inc (PEG), sold 2,083 shares of the company on March 5, 2026. This transaction increased his total holdings to 292,889.0003 shares. Over the past year, Larossa has sold 7,604 shares with no purchases, and the company has seen seven insider sells and no buys.

feed.title: Skyworks Solutions: The Quiet Chip Powerhouse Wall Street Is Watching
feed.summary: Skyworks Solutions, an Apple supplier and semiconductor company, is being watched by Wall Street as a potential comeback story due to its integral role in 5G, Wi-Fi 7, and connected devices despite being a "quiet" player. The article discusses its exposure to Apple, dividend, cash flow, and valuation compared to AI giants, while also acknowledging risks like dependence on Apple and competition. For investors, Skyworks represents a way to play connectivity growth without the volatility of highly hyped tech stocks.

feed.title: Lamb Weston (LW) to Join S&P 600 in Upcoming Index Rebalance
feed.summary: Lamb Weston (LW), a leading producer of frozen potato products, is set to join the S&P 600 index before market open on Monday, March 23, 2026, as part of a routine quarterly rebalancing. This inclusion acknowledges the company's strong market performance and potentially influences investor perception. While Lamb Weston demonstrates impressive financial performance with significant revenue growth and profitability, concerns exist regarding its debt levels and asset efficiency.

feed.title: Institutional Shifts Challenge Consensus On Equity Residential’s Earnings Outlook
feed.summary: Institutional investors are showing mixed signals regarding Equity Residential (NYSE:EQR), with some like APG Asset Management US Inc. and Erste Asset Management GmbH reducing stakes, while Dimensional Fund Advisors LP has increased its position. This, coupled with split analyst activity, suggests a lack of clear consensus on the company's future earnings outlook. Shareholders are advised to scrutinize their own investment thesis, considering factors like rental markets, balance sheet strength, and income stability as the company prepares to address these cross-currents at an upcoming conference.

feed.title: Capital One to eliminate 1,139 jobs connected with former Discover facility in Riverwoods
feed.summary: Capital One plans to eliminate 1,139 jobs associated with the former Discover Financial facility in Riverwoods, Illinois, following its merger with Discover. These job cuts include employees working at the site, as well as remote Illinois and out-of-state residents reporting to Riverwoods-based teams. The company stated that customer-facing roles are not affected and is providing support to impacted employees, with separations expected to begin in May 2026.

feed.title: Insider Selling: Public Service Enterprise Group (NYSE:PEG) CEO Sells 2,083 Shares of Stock
feed.summary: Public Service Enterprise Group (NYSE:PEG) CEO Ralph Larossa sold 2,083 shares of the company's stock for approximately $174,264, reducing his total stake by 0.71%. This transaction follows the company's recent strong performance, including a slight quarterly EPS beat, 18.3% revenue increase, raised FY2026 guidance, and an increased quarterly dividend. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with a consensus price target of $92.73.

feed.title: 3 Defense Stocks Under $20 With Massive Upside
feed.summary: This article highlights three small-cap defense stocks—Unusual Machines (UMAC), Inseego (INSG), and SES AI (SES)—that offer significant upside potential for investors. These companies operate in high-growth areas such as military drones, battlefield connectivity, and next-generation batteries, with each providing "picks-and-shovels" technology crucial for defense modernization. Despite recent surges in the defense sector, these stocks trading under $20 still present an attractive investment opportunity by supporting the broader defense industry.

feed.title: CSX ‘addressing’ Atlanta bridge where driver reports rocks are falling into traffic
feed.summary: A driver in Atlanta reported that rocks fell from a CSX railroad bridge, shattering his windshield, an incident he believes was caused by a moving train. After nearly a month without a response from CSX, the driver contacted the media, leading to a quick resolution and reimbursement for the damages. CSX confirmed they are addressing the issue, emphasizing safety as their top priority.

feed.title: Bunge Global SA (US12185T1043): What the Latest Market Moves Mean for Global Agribusiness Investors
feed.summary: This article analyzes Bunge Global SA's current market position, recent news, and macroeconomic factors impacting its outlook. It delves into the company's business model, global footprint, financial performance, and technical chart analysis. The report also highlights key risks, ESG considerations, and the company's role for global investors looking into 2026.

feed.title: Vertiv, Lumentum, Coherent, EchoStar set to join S&P 500
feed.summary: S&P Dow Jones Indices announced that Vertiv, Lumentum, Coherent, and EchoStar will be added to the S&P 500 index on March 23, replacing Match Group, Molina Healthcare, Lamb Weston, and Paycom. This change typically leads to a stock price increase for new entrants as index-tracking funds buy shares, while removed companies may experience selling pressure. Following the news, Vertiv and EchoStar shares jumped significantly in extended trading.

feed.title: 200 Texas workers will be laid off at Campbell’s plant as famed brand moves from producing soups to sauces
feed.summary: Campbell's will lay off approximately 200 workers at its Paris, Texas plant as it shifts production from soups to sauces like Prego and Pace salsa. This move finalizes a decision announced in July 2024 to转型 the facility into a flagship sauce plant. Although the change impacts fewer roles than initially anticipated, the company is also closing its Cape Cod chips plant in Massachusetts, leading to nearly 50 additional job losses.

feed.title: Port Arthur LNG Announces Full Tuition Scholarship Program for Southeast Texas Students
feed.summary: Port Arthur LNG has launched the Pathways to Port Arthur LNG Scholarship Fund in collaboration with the Foundation for Southeast Texas. This program will provide ten graduating high school seniors from Jefferson and Orange Counties with up to four years of full tuition, books, and fees for STEM degrees at eligible Southeast Texas institutions annually. The initiative aims to remove financial barriers, strengthen the future workforce, and increase economic opportunity in the region.

feed.title: InMed Pharmaceuticals Shifts Focus to Therapeutic Programs, Ends BayMedica’s Non-Pharmaceutical Cannabinoid Sales
feed.summary: InMed Pharmaceuticals has announced a strategic shift to prioritize its pharmaceutical development pipeline, discontinuing the commercialization of non-pharmaceutical cannabinoid products under BayMedica. This decision aims to reallocate resources towards core pharmaceutical research and development, particularly focusing on treatments for dermatological and neurological conditions using rare cannabinoids. The move reflects InMed's commitment to advancing its clinical-stage programs and optimizing operational efficiency.

feed.title: Motorola Solutions (NYSE:MSI) CEO Sells $13,590,508.25 in Stock
feed.summary: Motorola Solutions' CEO, Gregory Brown, sold 28,435 shares of the company's stock for over $13.59 million on March 4th, reducing his stake by nearly 24%. This insider transaction follows strong quarterly earnings where the company exceeded revenue and EPS expectations. Motorola Solutions also declared a quarterly dividend of $1.21, and analysts maintain a consensus "Buy" rating on the stock.

feed.title: AbbVie (NYSE:ABBV) SVP David Ryan Purdue Sells 5,230 Shares
feed.summary: AbbVie Senior Vice President David Ryan Purdue sold 5,230 shares of the company's stock, reducing his holding by over 66%. Despite this insider sale, AbbVie recently beat Q4 earnings estimates, raised its FY2026 EPS guidance, and declared a quarterly dividend. However, analysts have expressed concerns regarding the company's high payout ratio and increased leverage following an $8 billion senior notes offering.

feed.title: Fastenal (NASDAQ:FAST) Director Reyne Wisecup Sells 36,920 Shares
feed.summary: Fastenal Director Reyne Wisecup sold 36,920 shares of the company's stock on March 5th for approximately $1.75 million, reducing her position by 48%. The sale leaves her with 40,000 shares. This transaction follows Fastenal's recent quarterly earnings report, where it met consensus EPS estimates and announced an increase in its quarterly dividend.

feed.title: Robert Muraro Sells 24,589 Shares of Targa Resources (NYSE:TRGP) Stock
feed.summary: Robert Muraro, an insider at Targa Resources (NYSE:TRGP), sold 24,589 shares of the company's stock on March 5th, 2026, for approximately $5.93 million, reducing his holding by over 11%. This transaction valued his remaining shares at around $47.64 million. Targa Resources recently exceeded Q4 EPS expectations but had slightly lower revenue, and currently holds a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts with an average price target of $240.79.

feed.title: CSX (NASDAQ:CSX) CEO Stephen Angel Buys 25,000 Shares of Stock
feed.summary: CSX CEO Stephen Angel purchased 25,000 shares of company stock for approximately $1.01 million, increasing his stake by over 20%. The company recently raised its quarterly dividend to $0.14 per share, but its latest earnings report missed analyst estimates. Despite the missed earnings, analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating for CSX.

feed.title: Boeing reportedly close to 500-jet order at US-China summit
feed.summary: Boeing is expected to secure an order for 500 737 Max jets during President Trump's upcoming visit to China. Additionally, the United States and China are in discussions for orders of approximately 100 787 Dreamliners and 777X planes. This potential deal highlights significant aerospace trade between the two nations.

feed.title: Insulet stock: Is the Omnipod maker your next stealth growth play?
feed.summary: Insulet, the maker of the Omnipod insulin pump, is gaining attention as a potential "stealth growth play" despite being overshadowed by GLP-1 weight loss drugs. The article explores why investors and users should consider Insulet, highlighting its recurring revenue model from consumable pods and the Omnipod 5's automated insulin delivery system. It also discusses the impact of GLP-1 drugs on Insulet's market, noting that while type 1 diabetes remains a strong core, type 2 presents a wild card for future growth.

feed.title: ROSEN, A LEADING LAW FIRM, Encourages Inovio Pharmaceuticals Inc. Investors to Secure Counsel ...
feed.summary: Rosen Law Firm is encouraging investors of Inovio Pharmaceuticals, Inc. (NASDAQ: INO) to secure legal counsel as they investigate potential securities claims. The firm highlights an upcoming lead plaintiff deadline of April 7, 2026, for investors who purchased INO securities between October 10, 2023, and December 26, 2025. The lawsuit alleges that Inovio made false and misleading statements regarding its manufacturing, regulatory prospects for its DNA medicine INO-3107, and overall commercial prospects.

feed.title: Henry Schein (NASDAQ:HSIC) Director Sells $228,543.84 in Stock
feed.summary: Henry Schein (NASDAQ:HSIC) Director Philip Laskawy sold 2,844 shares of the company's stock for $228,543.84, reducing his stake by 11.47%. This insider sale occurred after Henry Schein reported strong quarterly earnings, beating expectations with $1.34 EPS and $3.44 billion in revenue, and issued optimistic FY2026 guidance. Despite the positive earnings, shares traded down 3.2% to $77.60, and analysts currently hold a "Hold" rating with an average price target of $85.58.

feed.title: Insider Selling: Take-Two Interactive Software (NASDAQ:TTWO) Director Sells 1,390 Shares of Stock
feed.summary: Take-Two Interactive Software (NASDAQ:TTWO) Director Michael Dornemann sold 1,390 shares of the company's stock on March 5th at an average price of $213.09, totaling over $296,000. This transaction reduced his stake by 6.07% to 21,525 shares. Despite the insider selling, analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for TTWO with a consensus price target of $284.44.

feed.title: Insider Selling: STERIS (NYSE:STE) Director Sells 3,098 Shares of Stock
feed.summary: STERIS (NYSE:STE) Director Cynthia Feldmann sold 3,098 shares of the company's stock on March 5th at an average price of $237.21, totaling $734,876.58. This transaction reduced her ownership by 81.46% to 705 shares. The sale occurred on a day when STERIS shares traded down about 3.4% to $230.49 on heavier volume, following the company's Q results announcement of $2.53 EPS (meeting estimates) and a 9.2% increase in revenue.

feed.title: Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) CFO Sells $815,908.32 in Stock
feed.summary: Phillips 66 CFO Kevin Mitchell recently sold 4,944 shares of company stock for over $815,000, reducing his position by 4.83%. Despite this insider selling, the company increased its quarterly dividend and reported strong quarterly earnings that beat consensus estimates, although revenue slightly missed expectations. Phillips 66 shares are trading near their 52-week high, and analysts hold a mixed view with a consensus "Hold" rating.

feed.title: Lamb Weston (LW) to Exit S&P 500 in Upcoming Index Rebalance
feed.summary: Lamb Weston Holdings Inc. (LW) is slated for removal from the S&P 500 index during the upcoming quarterly rebalance, effective March 23. Despite this, the company, a leading producer of frozen potato products with a $6.39 billion market capitalization, reports robust revenue growth and solid profitability. While the exit may lead to short-term stock fluctuations, its fundamental strengths and market position are expected to help it navigate future challenges.

feed.title: Aptiv PLC Announces Cash Tender Offer for Senior Notes Including 3.250% Due 2032 and 5.150% Due 2034
feed.summary: Aptiv PLC has initiated a cash tender offer to repurchase several series of its outstanding senior notes, including those due in 2032 and 2034. This financial maneuver, announced on March 6, 2026, is part of Aptiv's ongoing financial management strategy. The offer allows noteholders to sell their notes back to the company before maturity, with specific terms disclosed in official communications.

feed.title: ROSEN, A LEADING LAW FIRM, Encourages Inovio Pharmaceuticals Inc. Investors to Secure Counsel Before Important Deadline in Securities Class Action – INO
feed.summary: Rosen Law Firm is encouraging investors of Inovio Pharmaceuticals, Inc. (NASDAQ: INO) who purchased securities between October 10, 2023, and December 26, 2025, to secure legal counsel. There is an important lead plaintiff deadline of April 7, 2026, for a securities class action lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that Inovio made false and misleading statements regarding its CELLECTRA device manufacturing, the likelihood of submitting the INO-3107 Biologics License Application, and the drug's prospects for FDA approval.

feed.title: Bronstein, Bank of America’s chief people officer, sells $2.99m in stock
feed.summary: Sheri B. Bronstein, Chief People Officer at Bank of America (NYSE:BAC), sold 60,000 shares of common stock for $2.99 million on March 5, 2026. The sales occurred at weighted average prices between $49.65 and $50.24, which is above the current stock trading price of $48.61. This transaction reduces Bronstein's direct ownership to 335,690 shares and comes as Bank of America engages in various strategic initiatives, including bond redemptions, an upcoming IPO for Mavis Tire, strategic partnerships, and increased CEO compensation.

feed.title: Cintas Just Won't Let UniFirst Go... And That Creates A Catalyst For You (NYSE:UNF)
feed.summary: UniFirst Corporation (UNF) is considered deeply undervalued, with rumors of a potential buyout by Cintas Corporation (CTAS) at over $275 per share, implying a significant near-term return. Despite short-term earnings weakness due to growth investments, UNF's long-term fundamentals and cost-saving potential make it an attractive investment, even without a buyout. The author maintains a Buy rating on UNF due to its valuation and optionality, while reaffirming a Sell rating on CTAS because of its premium pricing.

feed.title: According to the latest filing disclosed by the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), InMed Pharmaceuticals Inc (INM) officially confirmed and passed a resolution on March 4 to gradually terminate the commercial operations of its Baymedica divisio
feed.summary: InMed Pharmaceuticals Inc (INM) has officially resolved to gradually terminate the commercial operations of its Baymedica division, a decision confirmed by the latest SEC filing on March 4. This strategic shift indicates the company's intention to reallocate future resources towards its core business areas. The move aims to streamline operations and focus on key growth segments.

feed.title: InMed Pharmaceuticals Exits BayMedica to Refocus on Pipeline
feed.summary: InMed Pharmaceuticals (INM) announced its decision to wind down operations of its BayMedica subsidiary by June 30, 2026, due to the anticipated impact of H.R. 5371, which would prohibit aspects of BayMedica’s cannabinoid business. This strategic pivot will allow InMed to refocus exclusively on advancing its pharmaceutical pipeline, specifically drug candidates INM-901 for Alzheimer's and INM-089 for dry age-related macular degeneration. The company expects to incur approximately $670,000 in wind-down costs, partially offset by limited sales during the transition.

feed.title: InMed Provides Update on BayMedica Operations and Strengthens Focus on Pharmaceutical Development Pipeline
feed.summary: InMed Pharmaceuticals Inc. announced the wind-down of its wholly-owned subsidiary, BayMedica LLC's commercial operations, due to regulatory uncertainties surrounding U.S. federal legislation. This strategic decision will allow InMed to focus exclusively on advancing its core pharmaceutical drug development programs, INM-901 for Alzheimer's and INM-089 for dry age-related macular degeneration. The wind-down is expected to be substantially completed by June 30, 2026, incurring approximately $670,000 in severance and related expenditures.

feed.title: Jefferies Maintains Buy on JCAP Jefferson Capital Mar 2026
feed.summary: Jefferies has reiterated its Buy rating on Jefferson Capital, Inc. Common Stock (JCAP) with a $29.00 price target, citing expectations for a solid fourth quarter. This rating implies a potential upside of approximately 34% from current trading levels, despite a modest market reaction to the announcement. Meyka AI also rates JCAP with a B+ grade, considering various financial and market performance factors.

feed.title: Is JinkoSolar Holding (NYSE:JKS) Share Price Misaligned With Recent Valuation Signals
feed.summary: This article analyzes JinkoSolar Holding's (NYSE:JKS) share price using several valuation methods, including a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis and Price-to-Sales (P/S) ratio. Both methods suggest that JKS is currently undervalued, with the DCF model indicating a 72.8% discount to intrinsic value and the P/S ratio being significantly lower than industry and peer averages. The article also presents contrasting bullish and bearish "Narratives" from investors, offering different perspectives on the company's future revenue growth, margins, and policy risks.

feed.title: iQSTEL unveils corporate presentation outlining $430M 2026 revenue forecast and path to ~$15M EBITDA
feed.summary: iQSTEL (IQST) has released a corporate presentation forecasting $430 million in revenue by 2026 and projecting an EBITDA of $9-15 million at higher revenue levels, aiming for $25M at $1B. The company plans to achieve this through minority interest consolidation, EBITDA-accretive acquisitions, and expansion into AI and cybersecurity, seeking $20 million in capital over the next year to fund these initiatives.

feed.title: iQSTEL Enters Monetization Phase with Revenue Acceleration Plan
feed.summary: iQSTEL (IQST), a Nasdaq-listed telecom and tech group, has announced it is entering a monetization and EBITDA acceleration phase after completing its infrastructure build-out. The company projects $400 million in revenue run rate, $317 million for 2025, and $430 million for 2026, with an adjusted EBITDA run rate of $2.7 million. Despite these financial targets, TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates IQST as "Neutral" due to weak profitability, negative operating cash flow, poor ROE, and dilution risks.

feed.title: President Donald Trump to visit Cincinnati to tout push for lower drug prices
feed.summary: President Donald Trump is scheduled to visit Greater Cincinnati next week, specifically ThermoFisher Scientific in Reading, to promote his administration's efforts to lower prescription drug prices. This visit is part of a larger initiative, which includes the recently launched TrumpRx website, designed to help Americans purchase discounted medications directly from manufacturers. The administration aims to address the rising cost of living, a key concern for voters ahead of the November midterm elections.

feed.title: Starbucks to close store in Seattle's University District
feed.summary: Starbucks plans to close its University District store in Seattle on April 5th, following a three-month strike. This closure is part of a larger $1 billion turnaround plan the coffee company is implementing. The announcement was made by Egan Ward for the Puget Sound Business Journal.

feed.title: 2 Lesser Discussed AI Stocks Worth Keeping a Close Eye On
feed.summary: While NVIDIA and other prominent AI stocks often dominate discussions, Photronics (PLAB) and Teradyne (TER) are two lesser-discussed companies playing critical roles in the AI ecosystem. Photronics is a leader in photomasks essential for chip manufacturing, benefiting as AI chips become more complex, while Teradyne manufactures equipment to test these increasingly intricate AI chips. Both companies have positive EPS outlooks, despite recent stock weakness, and are positioned for growth in the expanding AI industry.

feed.title: Economic implications of war: Middle East war and the automotive industry
feed.summary: This article analyzes the potential economic impact of the ongoing war in the Middle East on the automotive industry, categorizing the effects based on the conflict's duration: short-term, midterm, and longer-term scenarios. It details how oil prices, shipping costs, vehicle production, and consumer demand for different vehicle types would be affected. S&P Global Mobility currently maintains a base-case presumption of a short-term conflict for its vehicle production and sales forecasts, but acknowledges the significant uncertainties.

feed.title: Intuitive Surgical, Inc. (ISRG) Stock Moves -1.32%: What You Should Know
feed.summary: The article reports that Intuitive Surgical, Inc. (ISRG) stock experienced a -1.32% change. It focuses on the stock's performance rather than providing comprehensive company news or financial analysis. The text largely consists of privacy policy information.

feed.title: Eversource Statement on Proposed Final Decision to Approve Aquarion Sale
feed.summary: Eversource Energy has released a statement regarding the Connecticut Public Utilities Regulatory Authority's (PURA) proposed final decision to approve the sale of Aquarion Water Company. While the decision is viewed as constructive, Eversource is still considering the conditions, noting that the sale is not factored into their 2026 guidance as they have already taken steps to ensure stability with or without the sale. The company awaits PURA's final decision, scheduled for March 25th, and maintains its 2026 earnings projections and long-term earnings per share growth guidance, which assumed Aquarion would remain part of Eversource.

feed.title: Consolidated Edison Equity Offering Reshapes Capital Structure And Valuation Profile
feed.summary: Consolidated Edison (NYSE:ED) has completed a follow-on equity offering to increase its equity base and adjust its capital structure, a material event for investors. This offering provides fresh capital for supporting grid reliability and system upgrades, but also raises questions about dilution and future capital allocation for existing shareholders. The stock is currently trading near its estimated fair value, and its 30-day return suggests positive sentiment around the offering.

feed.title: Insider Selling: Quest Diagnostics (NYSE:DGX) EVP Sells $1,146,115.18 in Stock
feed.summary: Quest Diagnostics (NYSE:DGX) EVP Catherine Doherty sold 5,558 shares for $1,146,115.18, reducing her stake by 7.65%. The sale occurred after Quest Diagnostics beat Q4 expectations, set strong FY2026 guidance, approved a $1.0 billion share repurchase program, and increased its quarterly dividend to $0.86. Analysts currently rate the stock as a "Moderate Buy."

feed.title: Insider Selling: Quest Diagnostics (NYSE:DGX) SVP Sells 3,878 Shares of Stock
feed.summary: Quest Diagnostics (NYSE:DGX) SVP Michael Prevoznik sold 3,878 shares of the company's stock for approximately $799,682 on March 5th, reducing his stake by 9.36%. The sale followed Quest Diagnostics beating Q4 2025 EPS estimates and announcing a $1.00 billion share repurchase program and an increased quarterly dividend of $0.86. Analysts currently have a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating for DGX with an average target price of $210.29.
